Extract text, forms, tables, signatures,: Extract text, forms, tables, signatures, and structured data from documents using AWS Textract.
Authenticate with AWS using your Access: Authenticate with AWS using your Access Key ID and Secret Access Key. The IAM user or role must have permissions for AWS Textract (e.g., the `AmazonTextractFullAccess` managed policy).
Extract text, forms (key-value pairs), t: Extract text, forms (key-value pairs), tables, and signatures from a document. Supports JPEG, PNG, PDF, and TIFF. For multi-page PDFs, use Start Document Analysis instead.
Extract plain text from a document. Fast: Extract plain text from a document. Faster and cheaper than Analyze Document — use this when you only need the text content without forms or tables. Supports JPEG, PNG, PDF (single page), and TIFF.
Extract structured data from receipts an: Extract structured data from receipts and invoices: vendor name, totals, tax, line items, and more. Works best with clearly formatted receipts and invoices.
Extract structured data from identity do: Extract structured data from identity documents such as driver's licenses and passports. Returns fields like name, date of birth, ID number, and expiry date.
Start an asynchronous analysis job for a: Start an asynchronous analysis job for a multi-page document stored in S3. Returns a job ID that you can pass to the "Get Document Analysis Results" action to retrieve the extracted data once the job completes.
Retrieve the results of an asynchronous: Retrieve the results of an asynchronous document analysis job started with "Start Document Analysis". If the job is still running, the status will be IN_PROGRESS. For very large documents, results may be paginated — use the returned nextToken to fetch the next page.
The document to analyze. Supported forma: The document to analyze. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, PDF (single page), TIFF. Maximum 10 MB. Provide this OR the S3 fields below.
S3 bucket containing the document. Requi: S3 bucket containing the document. Required if no file is provided.
S3 object key (path) of the document. Re: S3 object key (path) of the document. Required if no file is provided.
Select what to extract. TABLES extracts: Select what to extract. TABLES extracts table data. FORMS extracts key-value pairs. SIGNATURES detects signatures.
Optional. Natural-language questions to: Optional. Natural-language questions to ask about the document (e.g. "What is the patient name?"). When provided, the QUERIES feature is automatically enabled.
The document to read. Supported formats:: The document to read. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, PDF (single page), TIFF. Maximum 10 MB. Provide this OR the S3 fields below.
The receipt or invoice to analyze. Suppo: The receipt or invoice to analyze. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, PDF (single page), TIFF. Maximum 10 MB. Provide this OR the S3 fields below.
The ID document to analyze. Supported fo: The ID document to analyze. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, PDF (single page), TIFF. Maximum 10 MB. Provide this OR the S3 fields below.
The S3 bucket containing the document to: The S3 bucket containing the document to analyze.
The S3 object key (path) of the document: The S3 object key (path) of the document to analyze. Supports multi-page PDFs and TIFF files.
Optional. A unique token to ensure the r: Optional. A unique token to ensure the request is not processed more than once. If the same token is reused within 5 minutes, the original job ID is returned.
Optional. S3 bucket where Textract will: Optional. S3 bucket where Textract will save the analysis output.
Optional. S3 key prefix for the output f: Optional. S3 key prefix for the output files.
The job ID returned by the \"Start Docum: The job ID returned by the "Start Document Analysis" action.
Optional. Pass the nextToken from a prev: Optional. Pass the nextToken from a previous call to retrieve the next page of results for large documents.
Maximum number of blocks to return per c: Maximum number of blocks to return per call (1–1000). Default is 1000.
